area: Governed commit / protected override audit deferral and lease renewal
finding: `git commit --auto-stage --defer-foreign-staged` could resolve a task-scoped bundle successfully while retaining pre-existing protected-override audit files in `commitFiles`; pre-commit then rejected those files as direction-lock scope drift. Long validation and the failed commit attempt could also expire the same actor's claim, forcing repair before retry.
expectedBehavior: Bundle resolution, index deferral, and hook scope checks must agree on whether pre-existing protected audit is task-owned or foreign; foreign audit must be deferred with a durable snapshot, while owned audit must be admitted by the active direction lock. Long evidence/commit operations must renew the active self-claim or expose an automatic retry-safe renewal.
evidenceOrRepro: TASK-AAO-0160 teaches governed commit ownership inference to read `taskId` from protected override audit JSON. Foreign protected audits can now be deferred out of the scoped commit, while current-task audit artifacts are admitted into the bundle. `validate:governance-commands` covers staged foreign audit plus task-owned audit and proves the commit includes only the deliverable and owned audit.
followUp: Keep the protected-audit deferral regression beside task-scoped staging coverage; separately retain lease-renewal diagnostics for long-running evidence/commit lanes.
